title: ferray-ufunc: no floating-point error state handling (errstate/seterr)
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy tracks floating-point errors (divide-by-zero, overflow, underflow, invalid) and allows users to control behavior via np.seterr() and np.errstate() context manager. The ufunc infrastructure checks FP exception flags after each operation (extobj.c). ferray-ufunc silently produces NaN/Inf without any error state tracking. While Rust's approach is generally to check for NaN explicitly, NumPy users expect to be able to catch division-by-zero as a warning or error. At minimum, document the difference. Optionally, add an error state mechanism for FP exceptions.
